phrases.intent_en: Ask about the main market location
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve le marché principal ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo seh TROOV luh mar-SHAY pran-see-PAL
phrases.cultural_note: The main market is at Place de la Palud (Wednesdays and Saturdays) and along Rue de la Mercerie. Local farmers and artisans sell here.

responses.english: At Place de la Palud, Wednesday and Saturday mornings.
responses.phonetic: ah lah PLAHS duh lah pah-LOO, mehr-kruh-DEE ay sahm-DEE mah-TAN
responses.local: À la Place de la Palud, mercredi et samedi matin.

phrases.cultural_note: Look for Läderach cheese, Swiss chocolate, honey from the Valais, and seasonal fruits. The region is known for wine (Lavaux vineyards nearby).

responses.english: Try the local cheese and Swiss chocolate.
responses.phonetic: goo-TAY luh froh-MAHZH lo-KAL ay luh shoh-koh-LAH SWEES
responses.local: Goûtez le fromage local et le chocolat suisse.

phrases.cultural_note: The Wednesday/Saturday market at Place de la Palud is primarily farmers and local producers. Arrive early for the best selection.

responses.english: Wednesday and Saturday mornings, at Place de la Palud.
responses.phonetic: mehr-kruh-DEE ay sahm-DEE mah-TAN, ah lah PLAHS duh lah pah-LOO
responses.local: Mercredi et samedi matin, à la Place de la Palud.

phrases.cultural_note: Most vendors accept cards, but cash is appreciated for small purchases. Have some Swiss francs on hand for the best experience.

responses.english: Most accept cards. Bring cash for small stands.
responses.phonetic: lah ploo-PAR ak-SEPT lay kart. pray-vwah-YAY doo lee-KEED poor lay puh-TEE stahnd
responses.local: La plupart acceptent les cartes. Prévoyez du liquide pour les petits stands.
